---
title: "SAE_Analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Show code by default in R Markdown output.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Core data manipulation and reshaping packages.
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(data.table)

# File input and output packages.
library(readxl)
library(gdata)
library(writexl)

# Date and clinical analysis packages.
library(lubridate)
library(comorbidity)
library(table1)

# BigQuery and database connection packages.
library(bigrquery)
library(DBI)
library(dbplyr)

# Modeling, reporting, and plot-composition packages.
library(broom)
library(patchwork)
library(mice)
```


```{r,echo=F}
# Load the SAE analysis dataset from the local CSV file.
data = fread("df_sae.csv")
```

## Data completeness for candidate risk factors

```{r data-completeness, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# List the candidate risk-factor variables to check before modeling hospital death.
candidate_risk_factors = c(
  # Demographic and admission characteristics.
  "race", "insurance", "language", "marital_status", "gender_f",

  # Laboratory values around SAE.
  "first_day_wbc", "wbc_before_sae", "wbc_after_sae",
  "first_day_hemoglobin", "hemoglobin_before_sae", "hemoglobin_after_sae",
  "first_day_platelet", "platelet_before_sae", "platelet_after_sae",
  "first_day_sodium", "sodium_before_sae", "sodium_after_sae",
  "first_day_potassium", "potassium_before_sae", "potassium_after_sae",
  "first_day_chloride", "chloride_before_sae", "chloride_after_sae",
  "first_day_bicarbonate", "bicarbonate_before_sae", "bicarbonate_after_sae",
  "first_day_bun", "bun_before_sae", "bun_after_sae",
  "first_day_creatinine", "creatinine_before_sae", "creatinine_after_sae",
  "first_day_glucose", "glucose_before_sae", "glucose_after_sae",
  "first_day_alt", "alt_before_sae", "alt_after_sae",
  "first_day_ast", "ast_before_sae", "ast_after_sae",
  "first_day_bilirubin_direct", "bilirubin_direct_before_sae",
  "bilirubin_direct_after_sae", "first_day_pt", "pt_before_sae", "pt_after_sae",
  "first_day_inr", "inr_before_sae", "inr_after_sae",
  "first_day_ptt", "ptt_before_sae", "ptt_after_sae",
  "first_day_fibrinogen", "fibrinogen_before_sae", "fibrinogen_after_sae",
  "first_day_d_dimer", "d_dimer_before_sae", "d_dimer_after_sae",

  # Respiratory support and treatment indicators around SAE.
  "first_day_invasive_vent", "invasive_vent_before_sae", "invasive_vent_after_sae",
  "first_day_noninvasive_vent", "noninvasive_vent_before_sae",
  "noninvasive_vent_after_sae", "first_day_hfnc", "hfnc_before_sae",
  "hfnc_after_sae", "first_day_norepinephrine", "norepinephrine_before_sae",
  "norepinephrine_after_sae", "first_day_epinephrine", "epinephrine_before_sae",
  "epinephrine_after_sae", "first_day_delirium_drug", "delirium_drug_before_sae",
  "delirium_drug_after_sae", "first_day_tracheostomy", "tracheostomy_before_sae",
  "tracheostomy_after_sae", "first_day_vasopressin", "vasopressin_before_sae",
  "vasopressin_after_sae", "first_day_phenylephrine", "phenylephrine_before_sae",
  "phenylephrine_after_sae", "first_day_dopamine", "dopamine_before_sae",
  "dopamine_after_sae"
)

# Keep only variables that are present in the dataset and flag any spelling issues.
available_risk_factors = intersect(candidate_risk_factors, names(data))
missing_from_dataset = setdiff(candidate_risk_factors, names(data))

if (length(missing_from_dataset) > 0) {
  warning(
    "These requested variables were not found in the dataset: ",
    paste(missing_from_dataset, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Count missing values for each variable; blank text values are also treated as missing.
missingness_table = data.table(
  variable = available_risk_factors,
  n_missing = sapply(data[, ..available_risk_factors], function(x) {
    sum(is.na(x) | (is.character(x) & trimws(x) == ""))
  })
) %>%
  mutate(
    n_total = nrow(data),
    percent_missing = round(100 * n_missing / n_total, 1),
    percent_complete = round(100 - percent_missing, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(percent_missing, n_missing, variable)

# Display variables ranked from lowest to highest missingness.
knitr::kable(missingness_table)

# Fill selected variables where missing has a clear clinical meaning.
zero_fill_variables = c(
  "first_day_hfnc", "first_day_invasive_vent", "first_day_noninvasive_vent",
  "first_day_tracheostomy", "hfnc_after_sae", "hfnc_before_sae",
  "invasive_vent_after_sae", "invasive_vent_before_sae",
  "noninvasive_vent_after_sae", "noninvasive_vent_before_sae",
  "tracheostomy_after_sae", "tracheostomy_before_sae",
  "first_day_vasopressin", "vasopressin_before_sae",
  "vasopressin_after_sae", "first_day_phenylephrine",
  "phenylephrine_before_sae", "phenylephrine_after_sae",
  "first_day_dopamine", "dopamine_before_sae", "dopamine_after_sae"
)

# Remove variables with more than 70% missingness, except variables filled by rules above.
variables_removed_high_missingness = missingness_table %>%
  filter(
    percent_missing > 70,
    !variable %in% c("language", zero_fill_variables)
  ) %>%
  pull(variable)

variables_for_imputation = setdiff(
  available_risk_factors,
  variables_removed_high_missingness
)

# Create a separate modeling dataset so the original raw data remain unchanged.
model_variables = c("hospital_death_flag", variables_for_imputation)
model_variables = intersect(model_variables, names(data))
sae_model_data = as.data.frame(data[, ..model_variables])

# Treat blank character values as missing before filling and imputation.
character_variables = names(sae_model_data)[sapply(sae_model_data, is.character)]
for (variable_name in character_variables) {
  sae_model_data[[variable_name]] = trimws(sae_model_data[[variable_name]])
  sae_model_data[[variable_name]][sae_model_data[[variable_name]] == ""] = NA
}

# Fill missing language values with Unknown.
if ("language" %in% names(sae_model_data)) {
  sae_model_data$language[is.na(sae_model_data$language)] = "Unknown"
}

# Fill missing respiratory support, tracheostomy, and vasopressor indicators with 0.
zero_fill_variables = intersect(zero_fill_variables, names(sae_model_data))
for (variable_name in zero_fill_variables) {
  sae_model_data[[variable_name]][is.na(sae_model_data[[variable_name]])] = 0
}

# Convert categorical variables to factors so mice uses categorical imputation methods.
character_variables = names(sae_model_data)[sapply(sae_model_data, is.character)]
sae_model_data[character_variables] = lapply(sae_model_data[character_variables], factor)

# Perform multiple imputation for remaining missing values.
imputation_method = mice::make.method(sae_model_data)
if ("hospital_death_flag" %in% names(imputation_method)) {
  imputation_method["hospital_death_flag"] = ""
}

# Use a compact predictor matrix to reduce instability from highly correlated before/after variables.
imputation_predictors = mice::quickpred(
  sae_model_data,
  mincor = 0.10,
  minpuc = 0.25,
  include = intersect("hospital_death_flag", names(sae_model_data))
)
if ("hospital_death_flag" %in% rownames(imputation_predictors)) {
  imputation_predictors["hospital_death_flag", ] = 0
}

set.seed(20260528)
sae_imputation = mice(
  sae_model_data,
  m = 5,
  method = imputation_method,
  predictorMatrix = imputation_predictors,
  maxit = 3,
  remove.collinear = FALSE,
  ridge = 1e-04,
  printFlag = FALSE
)

# Use the first completed dataset for downstream single-dataset modeling.
sae_model_data = mice::complete(sae_imputation, 1)
imputation_logged_events = sae_imputation$loggedEvents

# Confirm that the completed modeling dataset has no remaining missing values.
remaining_missingness = data.frame(
  variable = names(sae_model_data),
  n_missing = colSums(is.na(sae_model_data))
) %>%
  filter(n_missing > 0)

# Show which variables were removed before imputation due to high missingness.
knitr::kable(data.frame(variable_removed = variables_removed_high_missingness))

# Show any remaining missing values after rule-based filling and multiple imputation.
knitr::kable(remaining_missingness)
```
